title: Killers of the Flower Moon
logline: In 1920s Oklahoma, a series of brutal murders targeting wealthy Osage Nation members for their oil headrights exposes a conspiracy of greed that reaches the highest levels of white society.
protagonist: Mollie Burkhart (Osage woman whose family is systematically murdered)
antagonistOrConflict: Bill Hale and the conspiracy to murder Osage people for their oil wealth

actBreaks.description: Ernest and Mollie's wedding marks the end of setup and beginning of the systematic murder campaign.

validation.ruling: Scene #33 shows Minnie's death one year later, marking an irreversible shift that fundamentally changes the story's direction. This death consolidates more headrights toward Mollie and forces the story into higher-stakes conflict as the murder conspiracy intensifies.

actBreaks.description: Tom White's arrival and confrontation with Ernest shifts the story from conspiracy to investigation and reckoning.

validation.ruling: Scene #162 shows Ernest's interrogation by federal agents Tom White and Frank Smith about the murders of Reta, Bill Smith, and Anna Brown. This represents the major turning point where the federal investigation finally closes in on the conspiracy, forcing Ernest into a new phase of the story where he can no longer operate freely.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a methodical, sprawling crime epic that functions as both intimate family tragedy and sweeping indictment of American capitalism and racism. The structure mirrors the slow, systematic nature of the conspiracy itself - building relationships and trust in Act 1, then methodically destroying them through calculated murders in Act 2, before the inevitable reckoning in Act 3. The pacing is deliberately patient, allowing the horror to accumulate through repetition and escalation rather than sudden shocks.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script uses a slow-burn approach with short, interconnected scenes (average 1.2 pages) that create a mosaic effect. Key murders are spaced throughout to maintain tension, while domestic scenes with Mollie and Ernest provide emotional grounding. The investigation phase accelerates the pace through cross-cutting between multiple storylines converging on the conspiracy.

incitingIncident.description: A montage shows young Osage men dying mysteriously - John Whitehair, Bill Stepson, Anna Sanford, and Rose Lewis - all without investigation.

validation.ruling: Scene #33 is already identified as an act break and cannot be the inciting incident. Scene #32 serves as the true inciting incident - this montage shows young Osage men dying mysteriously without investigation, shattering the ordinary world and establishing the main conflict of systematic murder that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: At the Smith home, Reta tells Mollie she changed her will to protect her and suggests someone has put medicine on Mollie, questioning why she's helping the killers and urging her to leave for Colorado Springs.
midpoint.sceneHeading: 143 INT. BILL & RETA SMITH’S FAIRFAX HOME – NIGHT 143

validation.ruling: Scene #108 is the true midpoint where Reta confronts Mollie about helping the killers and urges her to leave for Colorado Springs. This represents Mollie's point of no return - she can no longer remain passive and must shift from reactive to proactive, marking a crucial internal transformation and raising the personal stakes significantly.

climax.description: Kelsie Morrison testifies that Hale hired him to kill Anna Brown for $1,600, describing how he shot her through the head in Three Mile Canyon.

validation.ruling: Scene #191 is the climax where Kelsie Morrison testifies in federal court about killing Anna Brown on Hale's orders. This is the peak confrontation that resolves the main story question, with the highest dramatic tension as the conspiracy is finally exposed in court with Mollie present to witness justice.

scenes.heading: INT. OSAGE LODGE MADE OF BARK CIRCA 1900
scenes.summary: Osage men and women gather in a traditional bark lodge for a ceremony where the Non-hon-zhin-ga holds a pipe and speaks about burying it tomorrow.

scenes.heading: EXT. OSAGE LODGE MADE OF BARK CIRCA 1900
scenes.summary: The Osage exit the lodge and bury the sacred pipe as newsreel footage introduces the oil-wealthy Osage Nation as the richest people per capita on earth.
scenes.turningPointNote: Establishes the central premise - the Osage's oil wealth that will drive the entire conspiracy

scenes.heading: 1 INT. TRAIN CAR – MOVING – SPRING, 1919 1
scenes.summary: Ernest Burkhart, a half-drunk WWI veteran, travels on a crowded train with soldiers, oil executives, and Osage families in spring 1919.

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. TRAIN DEPOT/FAIRFAX – DAY 2
scenes.summary: Ernest steps off the train at Fairfax depot and observes well-dressed Osage families while sketchy characters loiter nearby watching them hungrily.

scenes.heading: 3 INT. FANCY CAR – DRIVING 3
scenes.summary: Ernest rides with Henry Roan through oil derrick fields, learning that the land belongs to Henry.

scenes.heading: 4 EXT. HALE’S RANCH – DAY 4
scenes.summary: Ernest arrives at Hale's cattle ranch where his uncle William Hale greets him warmly with his wife and daughter.

scenes.heading: 5 INT. HALE’S RANCH – LIVING ROOM 5
scenes.summary: Hale tells Ernest by the fire that the Osage are sick people whose oil wealth will run dry, revealing his racist views and asking about Ernest's war service.

scenes.heading: 6 INT - BUNKHOUSE TYPE OF PLACE 6
scenes.summary: An Osage man writhes in agony and convulses on a bed, with boots and a jug visible nearby.

scenes.heading: 7 INT. PITTS BEATY’S BUSINESS – DAY 7
scenes.summary: Mollie Kyle meets with her white guardian Pitts Beaty to request money for a medical bill, identifying herself as an incompetent.

scenes.heading: 8 EXT. FAIRFAX STREETS – DAY 8
scenes.summary: Osage people line up outside a bank to deposit their quarterly annuity checks, with restricted and unrestricted members in separate lines.

scenes.heading: 9 EXT. CAR SHOWROOM - LOOKING DOWN AT A MAN, SALESMAN, ON HIS 9
scenes.summary: A car salesman begs three Osage men on his knees for a sale, and they choose a black Pierce Arrow to match a blanket color.

scenes.heading: 13 EXT. CATHOLIC CHURCH/FAIRFAX STREET – DAY 13
scenes.summary: Ernest waits in his taxi outside a Catholic church as Mollie exits and gets into his car.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. BILL HALE’S RANCH - LUNCHTIME 14
scenes.summary: Hale asks Ernest about Mollie Kyle over lunch, suggesting Ernest could pursue her since she's no longer with Matt Williams.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. ERNEST’S TAXI 15
scenes.summary: Ernest asks Mollie about her past relationship with Matt Williams while driving her in his taxi.

scenes.heading: 16 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME 16
scenes.summary: Ernest drops Mollie off at her home and watches her go inside as he begins reading about Osage history.

scenes.heading: 18 INT. BUNKHOUSE/HALE RANCH – DUSK 18
scenes.summary: Ernest reads about Osage culture in a bunkhouse when Byron arrives with Blackie Thompson to take him out for the night.

scenes.heading: 19 EXT. DENOYA HOUSE – FAIRFAX - NIGHT 19
scenes.summary: Three masked men including Ernest, Blackie, and Byron rob an Osage couple of their jewelry outside their home at night.

scenes.heading: 20 INT. BASEMENT/FAIRFAX – NIGHT 20
scenes.summary: Ernest bets the stolen jewels in a poker game and loses everything.

scenes.heading: 21 EXT. POOL HALL/FAIRFAX – DAWN 21
scenes.summary: Ernest and the others leave the pool hall at dawn, dejected after their losses.

scenes.heading: 22 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – DAWN 22
scenes.summary: Lizzie Q prays at dawn by a creek as Ernest narrates about Osage spiritual beliefs and the flower moon.

scenes.heading: 23 EXT. BRUSH ARBOR/OSAGE VILLAGE – SUNRISE 23
scenes.summary: Ernest watches from his car as Mollie participates in an Osage baby naming ceremony at sunrise.

scenes.heading: 24 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – ANOTHER DAY/AFTERNOON 24
scenes.summary: Ernest helps Mollie with shopping bags and she gives him a hat, then invites him to dinner.

scenes.heading: 25 INT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – THAT MOMENT 25
scenes.summary: Ernest enters Mollie's home and meets her traditional mother Lizzie Q, who stares at him without smiling.

scenes.heading: 26 INT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME - DINING ROOM 26
scenes.summary: Mollie and Ernest finish dinner and talk quietly about their living situations and families while Lizzie sleeps nearby.

scenes.heading: 27 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE'S GRAY HORSE HOME. 27
scenes.summary: Rain batters the exterior of Mollie's house.

scenes.heading: 28 INT. CATHOLIC CHURCH – DAYS LATER 28
scenes.summary: Ernest joins Mollie for Sunday church services and awkwardly stands when everyone else kneels, drawing looks from Bill Smith.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. CATHOLIC CHURCH – SAME DAY 29
scenes.summary: At a church picnic, Hale mingles with Osage men while Mollie's sisters discuss Ernest in Osage, calling him handsome but not smart.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. CAR – DUSK 31
scenes.summary: Ernest and Mollie make out in his parked car where he proposes marriage and she accepts.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ernest and Mollie's engagement sets up the central relationship that will be exploited in the conspiracy

scenes.heading: 32 SOMEWHERE 32
scenes.summary: Hale asks Ernest intimate questions about his relationship with Mollie and approves of the marriage.

scenes.heading: 33 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – DAY – SUMMER 1919 33
scenes.summary: Mollie and Ernest have a traditional Osage wedding ceremony witnessed by both communities, with Mollie presiding over a giveaway.

scenes.heading: 34 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – LATER 34
scenes.summary: At the wedding reception, Hale approaches the sickly Minnie and her husband Bill Smith with apparent concern but underlying tension.

scenes.heading: 35 B/W HOMES MOVIES OF OSAGE 35
scenes.summary: A montage shows young Osage men dying mysteriously - John Whitehair, Bill Stepson, Anna Sanford, and Rose Lewis - all without investigation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Establishes the pattern of Osage deaths that forms the core conspiracy

scenes.heading: 44 INT. MINNIE’S HOUSE - BEDROOM – DAY – 1 YEAR LATER 44
scenes.summary: Mollie's sister Minnie has died from a wasting illness, with the family mourning over her body.
scenes.turningPointNote: Minnie's death brings the conspiracy directly into Mollie's family

scenes.heading: 45 INT. MINNIE’S HOUSE - 45
scenes.summary: At Minnie's wake, Ernest waits in the living room while Bill Smith asks him to sit outside, creating tension between them.

scenes.heading: 46 EXT. MINNIE’S HOUSE 46
scenes.summary: Outside Minnie's house, Hale suggests to Ernest that Bill Smith didn't care for Minnie properly and that her headrights should go to Mollie.

scenes.heading: 48 BACK TO HALE - 48
scenes.summary: Hale tells Ernest that after Reta and Anna die, there will be Mollie, and discusses Anna carrying a gun in her purse.

scenes.heading: 50 HALE AND ERNEST LETTING THIS SETTLE 50
scenes.summary: Hale and Ernest let the implications of their conversation settle.

scenes.heading: 51 EXT. OIL FIELD 51
scenes.summary: Charles Whitehorn is shot and his body is dragged away and dumped in an oil field.

scenes.heading: 52 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME - DAWN 52
scenes.summary: Mollie and her daughter Elizabeth pray at dawn by a creek, then return to their home where fancy cars are parked outside.

scenes.heading: 53 INT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME - QUIET SPOT - MORNING 53
scenes.summary: Mollie tends to baby James by blowing smoke in his ear while she and Ernest tell their children a Coyote story.

scenes.heading: 56 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – LATER 56
scenes.summary: Anna arrives at Mollie's house clearly drunk in the middle of the day, carrying blankets for their mother.

scenes.heading: 57 INT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – THAT MOMENT 57
scenes.summary: Inside the house, the drunk Anna confronts Byron aggressively, making threats while the family watches uncomfortably.

scenes.heading: 58 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – DAY 58
scenes.summary: Byron and Ernest smoke outside and discuss Charlie Whitehorn's death, with Byron saying it's time to take Anna home.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME - LIZZIE’S BEDROOM – 59
scenes.summary: Anna sobers up while lying with her mother Lizzie, who calls her wild but her favorite blessing before Ernest bangs on the door.

scenes.heading: 61 EXT. BYRON’S CAR 61
scenes.summary: Ernest helps Anna into Byron's car as she asks for another drink, and Mollie watches anxiously as they drive toward Fairfax.

scenes.heading: 70 INT. ROUNDHOUSE - TRIBAL COUNCIL EMERGENCY MEETING – DAY 70
scenes.summary: Chief Bonnicastle leads an emergency tribal council meeting about recent murders, with Mollie announcing she has hired a private investigator.
scenes.turningPointNote: Anna's death (implied by the sheriff finding Mollie) escalates the crisis and brings official attention to the murders

scenes.heading: 73 BACK TO TRIBAL COUNCIL MEETING - 73
scenes.summary: Paul Red Eagle tells the tribal council they cannot depend on officials and must clean up their homes, while Hale offers a $1000 reward.

scenes.heading: 74 EXT. FURNITURE STORE/UNDERTAKER – FAIRFAX – DAY 74
scenes.summary: Ernest and Mollie wait outside the undertaker while Reta approaches with Bill Smith, who explains Reta comforted him after Minnie's death.

scenes.heading: 75 INT. FURNITURE STORE/BACK ROOM UNDERTAKER – DAY 75
scenes.summary: Ernest argues with the undertaker about inflated funeral costs for Anna, telling him to leave the jewelry alone this time.

scenes.heading: 76 INT. BIG HILL TRADING COMPANY - SCOTT MATHIS OFFICE - DAY 76
scenes.summary: Scott Mathis executes Anna's will, revealing her $100,000 estate goes to Lizzie, Mollie, and Reta, with Hale waiving his company's fee.

scenes.heading: 77 EXT. FAIRFAX STREETS – TRAIN DEPOT – DAY 77
scenes.summary: Mollie walks through crowded Fairfax streets guided by Ernest, seeing endless white faces as she voices her inner turmoil in Osage about evil surrounding her heart.

scenes.heading: 78 INT. - CAFE 78
scenes.summary: William J Burns tells Mollie and Ernest that no sheriff's department has investigated Anna's place, though he found her alligator purse there, confirming Byron brought her home.

scenes.heading: 79 EXT. FAIRFAX TOWN – FLASHBACK DAY 79
scenes.summary: Bill and Reta Smith exit Sheriff Freas' office as Burns explains Smith is conducting his own investigation into Anna's death.

scenes.heading: 80 BILL AND RETA TALKING TO THE CAB DRIVER THAT DROVE ANNA THE 80
scenes.summary: Bill Smith questions the cab driver who drove Anna, learning they went to the graveyard past Florers so she could look at her land and visit her father.

scenes.heading: 81 INT. CAB – DAY - FLASHBACK 81
scenes.summary: Anna sits in the cab at Gray Horse Cemetery, takes a last drink from Hale's flask, fixes her hair, and reveals to the driver that she was pregnant.

scenes.heading: 82 EXT. GRAY HORSE CEMETERY – FLASHBACK 82
scenes.summary: Anna stumbles across the cemetery graves and places flowers at her father's headstone.

scenes.heading: 83 INT. CAFE - CONTINUED 83
scenes.summary: Burns asks Mollie if she knew Anna was pregnant, but Mollie doesn't answer.

scenes.heading: 84 INT. POOL HALL – DAY 84
scenes.summary: Ernest reports to Hale at the pool hall that Anna was pregnant, and Hale tells him to steer clear of any connection to Byron or himself.

scenes.heading: 85 EXT REX MOVIE THEATER 85
scenes.summary: Bill and Reta visit the Rex Movie Theater asking questions while Byron Burkhart watches their investigation from inside a shop window.

scenes.heading: 86 INT. CAFE – CONTINUED 86
scenes.summary: Ernest asks Burns what Smith has found out, but Burns admits he doesn't know.

scenes.heading: 87 INT. POOL HALL – DAY 87
scenes.summary: Hale asks Ernest the same question about Smith's findings, and Ernest says he'll go see Blackie about what they discussed.

scenes.heading: 88 EXT. GRAY HORSE CEMETERY – NIGHT 88
scenes.summary: An explosion occurs at Gray Horse Cemetery as Blackie and another figure run from behind a gravestone.

scenes.heading: 89 OMITTED 89
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: EXT. OSAGE CAVES – DAY
scenes.summary: Ernest rides a horse to cave area and asks a group of outlaws where Blackie is.

scenes.heading: INT. A CRIMINAL HANGOUT AMIDST CAVES, a spot to stash stolen
scenes.summary: Ernest meets Blackie in a criminal cave hideout and proposes killing Bill Smith and Reta for their estate, describing it as a profitable partnership with Hale.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ernest explicitly proposes murder for financial gain, escalating the conspiracy

scenes.heading: 92 EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY 92
scenes.summary: Police chase Blackie across the prairie as Ernest's car is stopped on the highway, while a family portrait is taken of Mollie, Ernest, Elizabeth and Baby Cowboy.

scenes.heading: 93 EXT. TRAIN DEPOT – DAY 93
scenes.summary: A young white man carries a small wooden box of insulin from the train depot through Fairfax and up to the bank's second floor.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. SHOUN’S OFFICE – DAY 95
scenes.summary: The Shoun brothers show Ernest the expensive insulin from Toronto that Hale arranged, then examine Mollie for diabetes and prepare to give her an injection.

scenes.heading: 97 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – DAY 97
scenes.summary: Bill Hale arrives unannounced at Mollie's home, honking repeatedly until Ernest comes out and gets in the car with him.

scenes.heading: 98 EXT. FAIRFAX – MASONIC HALL/BANK – DAY 98
scenes.summary: Hale and Ernest park at the Masonic Hall/Bank and Ernest follows Hale inside.

scenes.heading: 99 INT. MASONIC HALL – BACK ROOM – DAY 99
scenes.summary: Hale forces Ernest to kneel in the Masonic Hall back room with Byron present, interrogates him about making a deal with Blackie, and has Byron bring a ritual paddle to beat Ernest.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hale violently punishes Ernest, asserting dominance and control over the conspiracy

scenes.heading: 101 INT. SHOUN BROTHERS’ OFFICE - DAY 101
scenes.summary: Henry Roan undergoes a medical examination by the Shoun brothers while Hale jokes about killing him, revealing Roan needs to be insured as a formality.

scenes.heading: 102 INT. HALLWAY OUTSIDE SHOUN BROTHERS’ OFFICE 102
scenes.summary: Henry Roan tells Hale his guardian won't give him money for moonshine and expresses suicidal thoughts, while Hale advises him against self-harm and mentions his wife's infidelity.

scenes.heading: 103 INT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME - MOLLIE’S BEDROOM – DAY 103
scenes.summary: Mollie refuses insulin treatment from the Shoun brothers in her bedroom, speaking in Osage to Ernest who tries to convince her to accept the doctors.

scenes.heading: 106 INT. ROY BUNCH BUTCHER SHOP - DAY 106
scenes.summary: Henry Roan attacks Roy Bunch with a club at the butcher shop, accusing him of being with his wife, until Mary Roan separates them.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. BANK/FAIRFAX - DAY 107
scenes.summary: Henry Roan shouts at bank employees about wanting to spend his own money while Hale and Ernest arrive to handle the situation.

scenes.heading: 108 INT. HALE RANCH/LIVING ROOM – MOMENTS LATER 108
scenes.summary: Hale and Ernest lay unconscious Henry Roan on the floor at Hale's ranch, with Hale explaining he has a $25,000 insurance policy on Roan and needs him alive for one more year.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. SUMMER HOUSE - LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – DAY 110
scenes.summary: Mollie combs her dying mother Lizzie's hair in the summer house while the Shoun brothers lurk in the background, and Lizzie dies with her eyes open.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lizzie's death removes another family member and consolidates more wealth

scenes.heading: 111 INT. SUMMER HOUSE - WITH ANCESTORS 111
scenes.summary: Lizzie's ancestors appear as spirits to escort her to the heavens, then Hale and Ernest comfort Mollie and the children in the present.

scenes.heading: EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE'S GRAY HORSE HOME - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Mollie, Reta and a wailing relative mourn for Lizzie on the porch in the early morning.

scenes.heading: 112 EXT. GRAY HORSE CEMETERY – DAY 112
scenes.summary: A large crowd gathers at Gray Horse Cemetery for Lizzie's burial as a traditional Osage leader gives a blessing about the soul's passage to heaven.

scenes.heading: INT. MASONIC HALL LODGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hale and six Freemason brothers perform a Scottish Rite ritual of extinguishing candles while Mollie and Elizabeth pray by a stream, with Hale extinguishing the final light.

scenes.heading: 113 EXT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – DUSK 113
scenes.summary: Hale visits Henry Grammer at his distillery and asks him to find someone who can kill an old man and his wife simultaneously using foolproof methods, not poison whiskey.

scenes.heading: 116 EXT. GRAMMER’S DISTILLERY 116
scenes.summary: Acie Kirby sets explosives in a bank vault, takes cover, and money rains down as the vault explodes, while Mollie tells Hale that Pitts Beaty's Klan is investigating Anna's murder.

scenes.heading: 117 INT. BANK – SOMEPLACE IN OKLAHOMA – DAY 117
scenes.summary: Barney McBride receives a telegram warning him to be careful, looks around suspiciously, and leaves the Elk's Club.

scenes.heading: INT. ELK'S CLUB - EVENING
scenes.summary: Someone seizes Barney McBride outside the Washington D.C. Elk's Club and ties a sack over his head.

scenes.heading: 119 INSERT: EXT. WASHINGTON D.C. ELK’S CLUB – EVENING 119
scenes.summary: Mollie announces she hired a private investigator.

scenes.heading: 120 INT. HALE'S RANCH - DINNER TABLE - CONTINUED 120
scenes.summary: Byron Burkhart and accomplices ambush private investigator William J Burns in his rooming house room, beat him unconscious, while Ernest rifles through his pockets and wallet.

scenes.heading: 121 INSERT: INT. FAIRFAX ROOMING HOUSE – NIGHT 121
scenes.summary: Hale questions Mollie about paying the private investigator and suggests he ran off, while Ernest announces that Mollie is pregnant.

scenes.heading: 122 INT. HALE'S RANCH – DINNER TABLE – CONTINUED 122
scenes.summary: Barney McBride is stabbed twenty times and his skull is beaten in while a bag covers his head in a D.C. alley.

scenes.heading: 123 INSERT: EXT. D.C. ELK’S CLUB ALLEY - MORNING 123
scenes.summary: Henry Roan arrives at Hale's ranch during the pregnancy celebration and asks to speak privately with Hale, while Ernest watches Mollie.

scenes.heading: 124 INT. HALE'S RANCH – NIGHT – CONTINUED 124
scenes.summary: Hale questions Ernest about being intimate with sick Mollie and discusses the pregnancy, while Ernest asks about Henry Roan's potential claim to Mollie's headrights and Hale confirms he's handling the threat.

scenes.heading: 125 INT. HALE'S RANCH – LIBRARY - LATER 125
scenes.summary: Ernest offers John Ramsey a job from Hale to kill someone, and when Ramsey initially refuses, Ernest specifies it's an Indian, which changes Ramsey's response.

scenes.heading: 126 EXT. GRAMMER’S DISTILLERY – MORNING – WINTER 126
scenes.summary: Ernest and John Ramsey cross the street through a KKK parade led by Pitts Beaty and enter a cafe where Ramsey meets Henry Roan.

scenes.heading: 127 EXT. OLD LADY RALL’S CAFÉ – FAIRFAX – DAY 127
scenes.summary: Oil workers watch from a distance as Ramsey and Roan retrieve whiskey from a disused oil well and sit drinking on a car's running board.

scenes.heading: 128 EXT. OIL RIG – 128
scenes.summary: Roan and Ramsey drink at Mrs. Mackie's speakeasy with a hooker while Mrs. Mackie cuts cocaine, as Ramsey builds courage for his planned action.

scenes.heading: 129 EXT. PRAIRIE 129
scenes.summary: Ramsey retrieves booze for Roan at their secret stash spot, then walks around the car and shoots Henry Roan in the back of the head while he's drinking.
scenes.turningPointNote: Henry Roan's murder eliminates a key witness and potential claimant to Osage wealth

scenes.heading: 131 INT. MRS MACKIE’S SPEAKEASY 131
scenes.summary: John Ramsey enters the pool hall shaky and glassy-eyed, finds Ernest who has Cowboy with him, and Ernest follows Ramsey to the telephone booth.

scenes.heading: 132 EXT. FIELD – DAY 132
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 133 INT. FAIRFAX POOL HALL – DAY 133
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 134 INT. POOL HALL TELEPHONE BOOTH – THAT MOMENT 134
scenes.summary: John Ramsey calls Ernest from a pool hall phone booth to report Henry Roan's murder is complete, hands Ernest the gun, and Ernest tells Mollie that Henry Roan is dead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Henry Roan's murder is completed, marking another successful killing in the conspiracy

scenes.heading: 136 INT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – DAY 136
scenes.summary: Pregnant Mollie returns home and Ernest watches her reaction as she asks if Henry killed himself, with Ernest lying that he wasn't murdered while Mollie recalls knowing Henry since childhood.

scenes.heading: 137 EXT. HALE’S RANCH – DUSK 137
scenes.summary: Hale angrily confronts Ernest outside his ranch about John Ramsey not shooting Henry Roan in the front of the head as instructed.

scenes.heading: 138 EXT. FAIRFAX/ERNEST’S CAR – DRIVING – NIGHT 138
scenes.summary: Ernest and Hale drive through Fairfax arguing about whether Ernest gave John Ramsey proper instructions, with Hale saying there's too much murder and telling Ernest not to swear on his children.

scenes.heading: 139 FAIRFAX RESIDENTIAL NEIGHBORHOOD IS LIT UP BY STRINGS OF 139
scenes.summary: Ernest and Hale drive through Fairfax neighborhoods lit up with strings of white lights to keep murder away, seeing paranoid Osage families packing to leave town.

scenes.heading: 141 EXT. BILL & RETA SMITH’S FAIRFAX HOME – NIGHT 141
scenes.summary: Bill Smith comes through his front door in the dark, moves forward slowly, and discovers a dead dog on his property.

scenes.heading: 142 EXT. BRUSH ARBOR - GRAY HORSE INDIAN VILLAGE – SUNRISE 142
scenes.summary: Months later at baby Anna's Osage naming ceremony, Mollie and Ernest attend with noticeably fewer Osage present than previous ceremonies, with family members including Elizabeth, Cowboy, Hale, and the Smiths.

scenes.heading: 143 INT. BILL & RETA SMITH’S FAIRFAX HOME – NIGHT 143
scenes.summary: At the Smith home, Reta tells Mollie she changed her will to protect her and suggests someone has put medicine on Mollie, questioning why she's helping the killers and urging her to leave for Colorado Springs.

scenes.heading: 145 INT. SHOUN’S OFFICE - SAME MOMENT 145
scenes.summary: Myrtle Hale calls James Shoun and tells him to get his best furniture out of the house.

scenes.heading: 146 EXT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME – DAY 146
scenes.summary: Ernest plays with Cowboy in the front yard when Hale arrives with Henry Grammer, instructing Ernest to find Acie Kirby and tell him it's time for the Smith job.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAIRFAX STREET AAA147
scenes.summary: Ernest finds John Ramsey and tells him to locate Acie Kirby for the Bill and Reta job, with Ramsey questioning why Ernest always gets others to do his work.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOMING HOUSE AA147
scenes.summary: John Ramsey enters a rooming house, knocks on Acie Kirby's door, and tells him it's time for the job, asking if he has his soup ready.

scenes.heading: INT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME – EVENING / NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ernest confronts Mollie when she returns from Reta's house, telling her not to go out so much and to keep the children inside, with Mollie resisting his controlling demands.

scenes.heading: 148 IN THE STREET 148
scenes.summary: Ernest walks toward a massive explosion site where Bill and Reta Smith's house has been blown into a hole in the ground, finding Bill Smith still alive and screaming to be shot, and Reta's body with her head partially destroyed.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Smith house bombing represents the most violent and public murder yet, escalating the conspiracy's brutality

scenes.heading: 150 EXT. STOCK SHOW – FT. WORTH, TX – THAT MOMENT 150
scenes.summary: At the exact moment of the bombing, Hale and Henry Grammer are at a stock show in Fort Worth, Texas, establishing their alibi as Grammer signs autographs and takes pictures with fans.

scenes.heading: 151 EXT. FAIRFAX – DAY 151
scenes.summary: A drone camera shows the crater where the Smith home once stood, with debris everywhere and people cleaning up as Hale and Grammer return to town, with Grammer noting they used too much dynamite.

scenes.heading: 153 INT. PITTS BEATY OFFICE – DAY 153
scenes.summary: Mollie meets with guardian Pitts Beaty requesting $300 to travel to Washington D.C., but he refuses the money and trip, saying it would be a disservice to her and her children.

scenes.heading: 154 EXT. TRAIN PLATFORM – FAIRFAX – MORNING 154
scenes.summary: The Osage Delegation departs for Washington D.C. by train, with Ernest helping the ailing Mollie board as she waves goodbye to him and the children.

scenes.heading: 155 EXT. WASHINGTON D.C., GOVERNMENT BUILDING – DAY 155
scenes.summary: Mollie stands next to President Coolidge for a photo op, then pleads with him to send help for the murders in Osage, telling him about her lost family members killed for oil money before he's ushered away.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mollie's direct appeal to the President sets federal intervention in motion

scenes.heading: 156 INT. SHOUN’S OFFICE – DAY 156
scenes.summary: The Shoun brothers prepare insulin for Mollie while Ernest, Hale, and Byron watch, with Hale privately telling Ernest the shots are just to slow her down.
scenes.turningPointNote: The conspiracy to poison Mollie with tainted insulin is revealed

scenes.heading: 157 INT. CATHOLIC CHURCH FAIRFAX – DAY 157
scenes.summary: Mollie, showing major health decline, tells a priest she's afraid to eat in her house, with the priest warning her about poisoned whiskey and the many deaths occurring.

scenes.heading: 158 INT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME – DAY 158
scenes.summary: Mollie holds a family meeting announcing she's sick, establishing strict rules about visitors, food preparation, and workers, while asking to move her bed downstairs and send baby Anna away for safety.

scenes.heading: 159 INT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME – LATER 159
scenes.summary: The Bighearts take baby Anna away as Mollie hands her over, with Ernest watching and the other children saying goodbye before Mollie isolates herself in the back room.

scenes.heading: 160 INT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME – AFTERNOON 160
scenes.summary: Mollie lies half-asleep in her downstairs bed when an owl walks into her room, followed by Ernest who approaches with an insulin needle and gives her the shot while they exchange loving looks.

scenes.heading: 161 OMITTED 161
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 162 CONTINUOUS - 162
scenes.summary: Mollie hallucinates hearing Osage dances and tells Ernest her mother came to dance with her and warned she was dying, then mentions seeing a man in a hat as FBI agent Tom White knocks on their door.
scenes.turningPointNote: The FBI arrives at Mollie's house as she's near death from poisoning, marking the federal investigation's direct intervention

scenes.heading: 166 EXT. HALE’S RANCH – DAY 166
scenes.summary: Hale examines his cattle at his ranch, appearing preoccupied while negotiating the sale of a large piece of his empire with CJ Robinson.

scenes.heading: 167 INT. FAIRFAX CAFÉ – DAY 167
scenes.summary: FBI agent Tom White meets with the Osage Tribal Council at a café, with Chief Bonnicastle asking about the lights in the streets and Paul Red Eagle questioning whether White's arrival is due to a white man's death or their $20,000 payment.

scenes.heading: 168 INT. INSURANCE OFFICE – DAY 168
scenes.summary: Hale loses his temper with insurance men Joe Jones and his partner, demanding his $25,000 payout for Henry Roan's death while they contest the payment and refer him to Denver.

scenes.heading: 169 INT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME - 169
scenes.summary: Ernest gives the delirious Mollie an injection as she speaks about Anna having no face and not being able to rest, with Ernest becoming scared when Mollie's head nods back into another world.

scenes.heading: 170 INT. SHOUN BROTHERS OFFICE - DAY 170
scenes.summary: FBI agents Frank Smith and John Burger question the Shoun brothers about Anna Brown's death, learning that inquest records are missing and the undertaker kept the skull, while the doctors claim they never found the bullet.

scenes.heading: 171 INT. FAIRFAX POOL HALL/BARBER SHOP - DAY 171
scenes.summary: FBI agent Tom White confronts Hale in a barber chair, with Hale suggesting the murders are committed by outsiders, possibly Black men, while positioning himself as the King of the Osage Hills.

scenes.heading: 172 INT. MOVIE THEATER – 172
scenes.summary: Hale quietly warns Blackie Thompson in a movie theater that government men have arrived and suggests he leave town, directing him to a specific location in Drumright.

scenes.heading: 173 EXT. DRUMRIGHT BANK – 173
scenes.summary: Blackie Thompson robs a bank in Drumright with three accomplices and his wife, shoots a police officer dead, and ends up handcuffed and arrested along with all his associates.

scenes.heading: 174 INT. BALLET SCHOOL/FAIRFAX – DAY 174
scenes.summary: Mostly Osage girls practice ballet exercises at a barre while Bob Mount thanks Bill Hale for sponsoring the dance school.

scenes.heading: 175 EXT. FAIRFAX STREET – DAY – SAME MOMENT 175
scenes.summary: Bill Hale gives away ponies to girls outside the ballet school while privately warning Bob Mount to watch his business tonight, as John Wren helps an Osage family search for something in an alley.

scenes.heading: 176 INT. BOARDING HOUSE – NIGHT 176
scenes.summary: Hale visits Acie Kirby at a boarding house, pays him $500, and suggests he take a trip since federal agents are investigating the blast.

scenes.heading: 177 EXT. ANOTHER TOWN - SHOP SIGN: “R MOUNT - GROCERY” 177
scenes.summary: Bob Mount waits with his shotgun at his grocery store and shoots Acie Kirby dead through the glass door when Acie and two associates attempt to break in.

scenes.heading: 178 EXT. HENRY GRAMMER’S RANCH/DISTILLERY – DAY 178
scenes.summary: FBI agents Tom White, John Burger, and Frank Smith discover Henry Grammer's hastily abandoned distillery with equipment knocked over but fires still burning, finding only an old timer who speaks cryptically about Grammer being with the snakes.

scenes.heading: 179 EXT. ROAD – NIGHT 179
scenes.summary: Henry Grammer drives with a strange expression on his face until his car smashes violently into a tree, killing him.

scenes.heading: 180 EXT. HENRY GRAMMER’S RANCH/DISTILLERY 180
scenes.summary: Agent John Burger asks the old timer when Henry Grammer's death happened, learning it occurred yesterday.

scenes.heading: 181 EXT. FAIRFAX POOL HALL – DAY 181
scenes.summary: Ernest plays pool with John Wren at the Fairfax pool hall, secretly drinking from a flask until Byron Burkhart arrives and tells him to put it away before they exit together.

scenes.heading: 182 EXT. BACK ALLEY WAY – DAY 182
scenes.summary: Byron leads Ernest to a car where Hale waits, telling Ernest he can beat any charges with the best lawyers, then pressures him to sign papers ensuring headrights stay in the family if something happens to him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hale secures legal control over the headrights while preparing Ernest for potential arrest

scenes.heading: 183 EXT FIELD WITH DERRICKS IN THE DISTANCE - NIGHT 183
scenes.summary: Joe Jones, CJ Robinson, and John Wren meet a group of men in a field with oil derricks in the distance, getting out of their cars and shaking hands.

scenes.heading: 184 EXT. FIELD - MOMENTS LATER 184
scenes.summary: Tom White huddles with his team and mentions meeting an old timer named Alvin Reynolds in Ralston the previous night.

scenes.heading: 185 EXT. RALSTON HOTEL – DUSK 185
scenes.summary: 84-year-old Alvin Reynolds tells Tom White on a hotel porch that he knew every murdered Indian and reveals the killers poisoned dogs and coyotes to test their methods, offering to identify who did it despite threats to his life.
scenes.turningPointNote: Key witness Alvin Reynolds provides crucial testimony about the conspiracy's methods and perpetrators

scenes.heading: 187 EXT. OIL FIELDS – NIGHT 187
scenes.summary: FBI agent John Burger reports to the team about Bill Stepson's death from corn whiskey and how Kelsie married Stepson's wife Tillie two weeks after Bill died.

scenes.heading: 188 INT. HOSPITAL – DAY 188
scenes.summary: Tillie lies dead in a hospital bed with Kelsie and her two children at her bedside, having died from poison two months after marrying Kelsie.

scenes.heading: 189 INT. LAWYER’S OFFICE – DAY 189
scenes.summary: Kelsie asks a lawyer if he would inherit his adopted Osage stepchildren's estates if they died, with the lawyer directly accusing him of planning to adopt and kill the children for their money.

scenes.heading: 190 EXT. POST OFFICE BOX – FT. WORTH, TX. 190
scenes.summary: FBI agents surround and arrest Kelsie Morrison at a Fort Worth post office, with Kelsie giving a false name of Lloyd Miller before being taken into custody.

scenes.heading: 191 EXT. OIL FIELD – BACK TO SCENE 191
scenes.summary: Burger tells Tom White that he asked Kelsie point blank about Byron, Ernest and the murders.

scenes.heading: 192 EXT - POST OFFICE 192
scenes.summary: Kelsie suggests to an undercover agent that maybe they can help each other out.

scenes.heading: 193 EXT. OIL FIELD – BACK TO SCENE 193
scenes.summary: John Wren reports that he's gained the trust of the suspects and Father Albert.

scenes.heading: 194 EXT. OSAGE GOLF COURSE – FLASHBACK - DAY 194
scenes.summary: At a golf course, the Priest tells undercover John Wren that a parishioner fears her life may be in danger.

scenes.heading: 195 EXT. OIL FIELDS 195
scenes.summary: John Wren tells Tom White there are more murders than what they're investigating and that convicting someone for killing an Indian is nearly impossible.

scenes.heading: 196 EXT. OIL FIELDS – CONTINUED 196
scenes.summary: The agents watch a fire in the distance at Bill Hale's ranch, and Joe Jones reveals he sold Hale a $30,000 fire policy last month.

scenes.heading: 197 EXT. HALE RANCH – NIGHT 197
scenes.summary: Bill Hale's ranch burns in a controlled insurance fire with ranch hands ensuring the blaze stays contained to specific areas.

scenes.heading: 198 EXT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME – NIGHT 198
scenes.summary: Ernest watches the fire on the horizon from outside Mollie's house as Tom White's voiceover describes Ernest as skittish and afraid.

scenes.heading: INT. CELLAR MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME
scenes.summary: Ernest mixes something with vials and whiskey in the cellar and drinks it.

scenes.heading: 199 INT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME – NIGHT 199
scenes.summary: Ernest, drunk and seeing flames like demons, injects the bedridden Mollie with two bottles while she tells him 'You're next.'

scenes.heading: 201 INT. FAIRFAX POOL HALL - FAIRFAX - DAY 201
scenes.summary: Tom White, Frank Smith and John Burger arrest Ernest at his pool hall despite his protests about needing to care for his sick wife.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ernest's arrest marks the beginning of the conspiracy's unraveling

scenes.heading: 202 INT. THE FEDERAL BUILDING, CIVIL SERVICE ROOM – NIGHT 202
scenes.summary: Tom White and Frank Smith interrogate Ernest for hours about the murders while he stands exhausted, eventually admitting he knows Blackie Thompson.

scenes.heading: 204 INT. CIVIL SERVICE ROOM – CONTINUED 204
scenes.summary: Blackie Thompson is brought in to confront Ernest, and they discuss what each told the agents about their involvement.

scenes.heading: 205 INT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME – NIGHT 205
scenes.summary: Bill Hale visits the dying Mollie in her bedroom, speaking to her in Osage and telling her he could be real.

scenes.heading: 206 INT. CIVIL SERVICE ROOM – LATER 206
scenes.summary: Tom White points out to Ernest that all the people he's blaming for the crimes are conveniently dead.

scenes.heading: 207 EXT. JOHN RAMSEY’S PLACE – RIPLEY 207
scenes.summary: John Wren arrests John Ramsey at his impoverished home in front of his wife and six children.

scenes.heading: 208 INT. CIVIL SERVICE ROOM – NIGHT 208
scenes.summary: Tom White interrogates John Ramsey about Henry Roan's murder, and when Ernest is brought in, Ramsey agrees to confess.

scenes.heading: 209 INT. MOLLIE’S FAIRFAX HOME – MORNING 209
scenes.summary: FBI agents find Mollie near death in her bedroom and rush her out of the house to get medical care.

scenes.heading: 210 INT. FEDERAL COURT BUILDING - HALLWAY 210
scenes.summary: Ernest tells Tom White that it's time for all the true facts to come out and requests protection from his Uncle.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ernest decides to cooperate and testify against Hale

scenes.heading: 211 INT. FAIRFAX SHERIFF’S OFFICE 211
scenes.summary: William Hale walks into the sheriff's office and calmly turns himself in for murder charges.

scenes.heading: 212 EXT. GUTHRIE FEDERAL BUILDING – DAY 212
scenes.summary: Under arrest, Hale tells reporters he won't try his case in newspapers but in the courts and maintains his composure.

scenes.heading: 213 INT. WITNESS INTERVIEW ROOM – DAY 213
scenes.summary: Prosecutor Leaward interviews Blackie Thompson, who confirms that Hale arranged the Drumright job that got him a life sentence.

scenes.heading: 214 INT. BLACKIE’S JAIL CELL - NIGHT 214
scenes.summary: A jail guard slips Blackie Thompson a note in his cell, and Blackie's voiceover reveals Hale promised to help him escape if he killed Ernest.

scenes.heading: 215 INT. WITNESS INTERVIEW ROOM – DAY 215
scenes.summary: Blackie Thompson sarcastically tells the prosecutor that Hale's promised escape obviously didn't work out since he's still in jail.

scenes.heading: 216 INT. HOSPITAL – PAWHUSKA – DAY 216
scenes.summary: Mollie is brought to a hospital room with doctors and nurses attending to her as Hale's voiceover begins reciting his poem.

scenes.heading: 217 OMITTED 217
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 218 INT. HALE’S JAIL CELL - GUTHRIE - DAY 218
scenes.summary: Hale dictates his poem 'Judge Not!' to a secretary in his jail cell.

scenes.heading: 219 EXT. FEDERAL PRISON - MORNING 219
scenes.summary: Ernest, under guard as a prosecution witness, asks to see his sick wife and tells reporters he's going the other way for a while.

scenes.heading: 220 INT. HOSPITAL - PAWHUSKA 220
scenes.summary: Mollie recovers in a guarded hospital room, eating and feeling better.

scenes.heading: 221 INT. HALE’S JAIL CELL – GUTHRIE – DAY 221
scenes.summary: Hale finishes dictating his poem and instructs that it be published in multiple local newspapers.

scenes.heading: 222 INT. HOSPITAL - PAWHUSKA 222
scenes.summary: Mollie, still under guard in the hospital, receives visitors Elizabeth and Cowboy as Ernest's letter is read in voiceover.

scenes.heading: 223 INT. FEDERAL COURTROOM - ENTRY DOOR TO COURTROOM 223
scenes.summary: Tom White tells the shaky and sweaty Ernest to hold steady before entering the courtroom.

scenes.heading: 224 INT. FEDERAL COURTROOM - CONTINUOUS 224
scenes.summary: Ernest enters the courtroom and sees Hale at the defendant's table, but Hale's lawyer immediately demands to confer privately with Ernest as his client.

scenes.heading: 225 EXT. WIDE OPEN SPACE – DUSK 225
scenes.summary: Ernest and Mollie meet on an empty highway under FBI surveillance, where Ernest apologizes and Mollie asks him to tell her he loves her.

scenes.heading: 226 OMITTED 226
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 227 OMITTED 227
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 228 INT. HALE’S RANCH - NIGHT 228
scenes.summary: At Hale's ranch, lawyers and oil executives pressure Ernest not to testify, warning him he's giving them the rope to hang himself.

scenes.heading: 229 INT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – MORNING 229
scenes.summary: Ernest watches Mollie give herself an insulin shot as she describes a dream about dumping his secrets in a river, and he tells her the situation is complicated.

scenes.heading: 230 INT. LIZZIE/MOLLIE’S GRAY HORSE HOME – MORNING 230
scenes.summary: Mollie watches from her window as Ernest is handcuffed and taken away by lawmen and lawyers.

scenes.heading: 231 INT. JAIL – DAY 231
scenes.summary: Ernest is brought to jail where Hale greets him with a long, silent bear hug.

scenes.heading: 232 INT. FEDERAL COURTROOM – DAY 232
scenes.summary: Kelsie Morrison testifies that Hale hired him to kill Anna Brown for $1,600, describing how he shot her through the head in Three Mile Canyon.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct testimony linking Hale to the murders

scenes.heading: 233 INT. MOLLIE’S BEDROOM – NIGHT – FLASHBACK 233
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Mollie bringing Byron a pillow and getting into bed with Ernest on the night of Anna's murder.

scenes.heading: 234 EXT. THREE MILE CREEK – FLASHBACK – NIGHT 234
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Byron propping up Anna's limp body as Kelsie aims his gun at the back of her head.

scenes.heading: 235 INT. JAIL CELL – NIGHT 235
scenes.summary: Tom White wakes Ernest in his cell to tell him that his child has died, and Ernest realizes it's baby Anna who had lung trouble.

scenes.heading: 236 INT. BIGHEART’S HOUSE - BEDROOM – DAY 236
scenes.summary: Mollie kneels in grief beside her dead daughter Anna, who lies wrapped in a blanket on the bed.

scenes.heading: 237 EXT. GRAY HORSE CEMETERY - DAY 237
scenes.summary: Ernest, in shackles and handcuffs, attends his daughter's burial with Mollie as the Catholic priest performs the service.

scenes.heading: 239 OMITTED 239
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 240 INT. JAIL CELL – NIGHT 240
scenes.summary: Tom White brings Ernest to see Hale in jail, where Ernest tells his uncle he has to testify, and Hale warns him the government won't hold to their deal.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ernest definitively chooses to testify against Hale despite his uncle's warnings

scenes.heading: 241 OMITTED 241
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 242 INT. FEDERAL COURTROOM – DAY 242
scenes.summary: Ernest testifies that he sought out John Ramsey at his Uncle William Hale's behest to arrange Henry Roan's murder, identifying Hale in court.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ernest's testimony directly implicates Hale in the murder conspiracy

scenes.heading: 243 OMITTED 243
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 244 INT. COURTHOUSE - LARGE ANTECHAMBER – DAY 244
scenes.summary: Mollie meets with Ernest in a courthouse antechamber where he tells her his soul is clean after confessing all truths and expresses relief at being out from under the conspiracy.

scenes.heading: 245 INT. RADIO BROADCAST STUDIO, NEW YORK - NIGHT, YEARS LATER 245
scenes.summary: A radio announcer declares that right had won as orchestra music swells and applause is heard.

scenes.heading: 246 INT. RADIO BROADCAST STUDIO, NEW YORK - NIGHT, YEARS LATER 246
scenes.summary: A radio broadcast concludes the authenticated story of the Osage Indian Murders, revealing that the Shoun Brothers were never pursued for poisoning Mollie due to insufficient evidence.
scenes.turningPointNote: Final resolution revealing that justice was incomplete - key perpetrators escaped prosecution

scenes.heading: EXT. OSAGE DRUM DANCE CELEBRATION - 2021
scenes.summary: Present-day Osage people gather in traditional and everyday clothing for a drum dance celebration, forming a colorful circle around drummers and singers.
scenes.turningPointNote: Epilogue showing Osage cultural survival and resilience in the present day

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
